$schema: Trellis migration adapter registry. Extensible — add custom tools here.

supabase.upDownNote: Forward-only SQL files. Manual repair via `supabase migration repair`.
supabase.rlsSyntax: Raw SQL: ALTER TABLE <t> ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY ...
supabase.fkSyntax: Raw SQL: FOREIGN KEY (col) REFERENCES table(col)
supabase.note: PostgreSQL under the hood. Pure SQL migration files.

prisma.upDownNote: Forward-only. Rollback requires manual `prisma migrate diff` to generate reverse SQL.
prisma.rlsSyntax: Not expressible in Prisma schema DSL. Must use raw SQL in migration.sql.
prisma.fkSyntax: Declarative: @relation(fields: [], references: []) in schema.prisma generates FK in SQL.
prisma.note: Migrations generated from schema.prisma. Each migration is a folder with migration.sql.

drizzle.upDownNote: Forward-only (as of 2025). Drizzle recommends dropping and re-baselining.
drizzle.rlsSyntax: Raw SQL in .sql files (TS schema has no RLS primitive).
drizzle.fkSyntax: Declarative: references(() => users.id) in TS schema generates FK in SQL.
drizzle.note: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite. Journal-based tracking via meta/_journal.json.

flyway.upDownNote: Forward-only in community edition. Undo (U prefix) requires Flyway Teams (paid).
flyway.fkSyntax: Raw SQL: ADD CONSTRAINT fk_... FOREIGN KEY (col) REFERENCES ...
flyway.note: Java/JVM ecosystem. Versioned (V) and repeatable (R) migrations.

alembic.upDownNote: Native. Each file has upgrade() and downgrade() functions. Revision graph via down_revision.
alembic.rlsSyntax: op.execute('ALTER TABLE <t> ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY') in upgrade() function.
alembic.note: Python/SQLAlchemy. Revision IDs are 12-char hex hashes.

rails.rlsSyntax: No native DSL. Use execute('ALTER TABLE... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY').
rails.note: Ruby on Rails. RLS requires custom SQL.

django.upDownNote: Forward-primary with limited reverse. Django infers reverse for most ops. RunSQL supports reverse_sql. Not all operations are reversible.
django.rlsSyntax: No native DSL. Use migrations.RunSQL(sql='ALTER TABLE...', reverse_sql='ALTER TABLE...').
django.fkSyntax: models.ForeignKey(..., on_delete=models.CASCADE) on model, auto in migration.

knex.upDownNote: Native. Each file exports { up(knex), down(knex) }.
knex.rlsSyntax: No native DSL. Use knex.raw('ALTER TABLE... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY').
knex.note: Node.js. RLS via raw SQL only.

golang-migrate.upDownNote: Native. Paired files: NNN_description.up.sql and NNN_description.down.sql (mandatory).
golang-migrate.fkSyntax: Raw SQL: ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY ...

typeorm.upDownNote: Native. Each class implements up(queryRunner) and down(queryRunner).
typeorm.rlsSyntax: No native DSL. Use queryRunner.query('ALTER TABLE... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY').
typeorm.fkSyntax: Entity @ManyToOne/@JoinColumn. In migrations: queryRunner.query('ALTER TABLE... ADD CONSTRAINT... FOREIGN KEY...').

goose.upDownNote: Native. SQL files use -- +goose Up / -- +goose Down annotations.
goose.rlsSyntax: Raw SQL in the +goose Up section.
goose.fkSyntax: Raw SQL in the +goose Up section.
goose.note: Go. SQL with annotation-based up/down sections.

atlas.upDownNote: Versioned mode: forward-only. Declarative mode (HCL): computes diff. Not a first-class down migration.
atlas.rlsSyntax: HCL has RLS DSL: policy {} blocks in schema definition. SQL mode: raw ALTER TABLE.
atlas.fkSyntax: HCL: foreign_key {} block. SQL mode: standard FOREIGN KEY.
atlas.note: Go. Schema-as-code. HCL is the only format with a first-class RLS DSL alongside node-pg-migrate.

sequelize.upDownNote: Native. Each file exports { up(queryInterface, Sequelize), down(queryInterface, Sequelize) }.
sequelize.rlsSyntax: No native DSL. Use queryInterface.sequelize.query('ALTER TABLE... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY').
sequelize.fkSyntax: queryInterface.addConstraint('posts', { type: 'foreign key', fields: ['user_id'], references: { table: 'users', field: 'id' } }).
sequelize.note: Node.js. Files use hyphen separator: YYYYMMDDHHmmss-<name>.js.

node-pg-migrate.upDownNote: Native. Each file exports { up(pgm), down(pgm) }.
node-pg-migrate.rlsSyntax: STAR FEATURE: pgm.enableRls('table_name') and pgm.createPolicy(...). Only tool with a dedicated RLS DSL API.
node-pg-migrate.fkSyntax: pgm.addConstraint('posts', 'fk_user', 'FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE').
node-pg-migrate.note: Node.js. PostgreSQL-native. The ONLY tool with first-class RLS DSL methods.

liquibase.upDownNote: Native rollback. rollback tag/SQL per changeset. Commands: rollback, rollbackCount, rollbackToDate.
liquibase.rlsSyntax: Raw SQL via <sql> changeset: ALTER TABLE... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY. No dedicated RLS changeset type.
liquibase.note: Java/JVM. XML/YAML/JSON/SQL changelogs. Trellis parses XML changesets for FK declarations.

defaults.message: No migration tool detected. Migration safety checks are skipped. Add a migration tool directory (see --list-adapters for supported paths) or create custom tooling.

disambiguation.description: When only .sql files exist with no config markers, use these heuristics:
rules: Single *.sql, no annotations, no markers → Custom/raw SQL (fallback)
